ops.body:
Bullet applied and then revised in the working tree (uncommitted, rides the pending 0.11.0 commit). Two review-driven changes for whoever lands 0.11.0:

1. **Session-isolation gap (confirmed in source):** the original bullet claimed each parallel agent gets "its own `--session <name>`" — impossible today. `bin/ab` keys its browser session off `cookiesync requestor` (bin/ab: `TAG="$(cookiesync requestor)"`), and the requestor derives from `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (cookie-sync internal/cli/requestor.go), shared by every agent in a session — so parallel agents all resolve to ONE ab session; `abwc-seed` has no session option. A per-agent `COOKIESYNC_REQUESTOR` override would isolate sessions but forfeit grant sharing (one tap per agent). The revised bullet states the pattern plus this precondition honestly. Real fix: give `ab`/`abwc-seed` a session override decoupled from the requestor (grant stays requestor-keyed, browser session gets its own name).
2. **Boundary wording:** the merged-session example previously included "a second dashboard", contradicting the fan-out bullet's N-dashboards example — revised to: merge co-dependent origins of one flow (app + API host, SSO chain); fan out hosts that stand alone.

getaway's own doctrine (shipped, targets released 0.10.0 where raw `agent-browser --session <name>` is the blessed interface) is unaffected until 0.11.0 releases; once it does, getaway's gather.md needs the abwc-seed-era commands AND the session override from (1). Also confirmed while reviewing: cookiesync caps `--reason` at 160 chars (internal/cookie/consent.go reasonCap) — getaway's doctrine now carries an overflow fallback; consider surfacing the cap in this skill's Notes too.
